response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong web and full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands 5–10 years of hands-on C++ development on Windows, yet Bogdan's C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE, plus a university thesis project using C++. His primary stack for the past decade has been TypeScript, Node.js, and web-oriented technologies. There is no evidence of capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge, and no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, Summit, STL, or any messaging framework relevant to capital markets infrastructure.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would transfer to any engineering environment
Java background (Altom Consulting) satisfies one of the optional desirable skills listed in the JD
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing history evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months) versus the 5–10 years required; the role's core technical mandate is essentially unmet
Zero demonstrated fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge, making neither of the two domain-substitution paths viable
No evidence of Windows-platform development, SQL Server/Oracle usage, STL proficiency, or capital-markets tooling (Summit, Kafka/MQ in a financial context)

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong engineering practices and CI/CD background
